title: Function expects file path, receives string content
error: Function returns 0 or empty when passed string instead of file path
cause: Function uses file operations (grep, cat, wc) on the argument, assuming it's a path. Passing content directly fails silently.
solution: Create a temporary file, write content to it, pass the path
example_bad:
count=$(count_real_errors "Error: something failed")
# Returns 0 - function tried to open file named 'Error: something failed'
example_good:
tmp_file=$(mktemp)
echo "Error: something failed" > "$tmp_file"
count=$(count_real_errors "$tmp_file")
rm -f "$tmp_file"
